title: Apparatus of the Crab
summary: A large iron barrel that unfolds into a crab-shaped submersible vehicle with multiple levers controlling its functions.
content:
## Apparatus of the Crab

*Wondrous item, legendary*

This item first appears to be a Large sealed iron barrel weighing 500 pounds. The barrel has a hidden catch, discoverable with a **DC 20 Intelligence (Investigation) check**. Releasing the catch unlocks a hatch at one end, allowing two Medium or smaller creatures to crawl inside. Ten levers line the interior, each in a neutral position, able to move up or down. When certain levers are used, the apparatus transforms to resemble a giant lobster.

The apparatus is a Large object with the following statistics:
- **AC** 20
- **HP** 200
- **Speed** 30 ft., swim 30 ft. (or 0 ft. for both if the legs and tail aren't extended)
- **Immunities:** Poison and psychic damage

The ten levers control the following:
1. Legs/tail extend or retract (movement)
2. Forward window shutter opens/closes
3. Side window shutters open/close
4. Claws extend or retract (melee, +8 to hit, 2d6 bludgeoning)
5. Each claw can be used as a grapple
6-10. Various functions (walk, turn, unclasping, etc.)

The apparatus can hold up to **10 hours of air** for two breathing creatures, and can submerge to a depth of 900 feet. Beyond that depth, the vessel takes 2d6 bludgeoning damage per minute.

title: Armor of Invulnerability
summary: Plate armor that grants resistance to nonmagical damage and can grant immunity to nonmagical damage once per day.
content:
## Armor of Invulnerability

*Armor (plate), legendary (requires attunement)*

You have **resistance to nonmagical damage** while you wear this armor. Additionally, you can use an action to make yourself **immune to nonmagical damage for 10 minutes** or until you are no longer wearing the armor. Once this special action is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

title: Belt of Giant Strength (Storm)
summary: A belt that sets your Strength score to 29 while worn, matching the might of a storm giant.
content:
## Belt of Giant Strength (Storm)

*Wondrous item, legendary (requires attunement)*

While wearing this belt, your **Strength score changes to 29**. If your Strength is already equal to or greater than the belt's score, the item has no effect on you.

| Type | Strength | Rarity |
|------|----------|--------|
| Hill | 21 | Rare |
| Stone/Frost | 23 | Very Rare |
| Fire | 25 | Very Rare |
| Cloud | 27 | Very Rare |
| **Storm** | **29** | **Legendary** |

title: Crystal Ball of Mind Reading
summary: A crystal ball of scrying that also allows you to cast detect thoughts on creatures you observe through it.
content:
## Crystal Ball of Mind Reading

*Wondrous item, legendary (requires attunement)*

This crystal ball is about 6 inches in diameter. While touching it, you can cast the **scrying** spell (save DC 17) with it.

You can use an action to cast the **detect thoughts** spell (save DC 17) while you are scrying with the crystal ball, targeting creatures you can see within 30 feet of the spell's sensor. You don't need to concentrate on this *detect thoughts* to maintain it during its duration, but it ends if *scrying* ends.

title: Crystal Ball of Telepathy
summary: A crystal ball of scrying that also allows telepathic communication and suggestion on observed creatures.
content:
## Crystal Ball of Telepathy

*Wondrous item, legendary (requires attunement)*

This crystal ball is about 6 inches in diameter. While touching it, you can cast the **scrying** spell (save DC 17) with it.

While scrying with the crystal ball, you can communicate **telepathically** with creatures you can see within 30 feet of the spell's sensor. You can also use an action to cast the **suggestion** spell (save DC 17) through the sensor on one of those creatures. You don't need to concentrate on this *suggestion* to maintain it during its duration, but it ends if *scrying* ends. Once used, the suggestion power of the crystal ball can't be used again until the next dawn.

title: Crystal Ball of True Seeing
summary: A crystal ball of scrying that grants true seeing through the scrying sensor.
content:
## Crystal Ball of True Seeing

*Wondrous item, legendary (requires attunement)*

This crystal ball is about 6 inches in diameter. While touching it, you can cast the **scrying** spell (save DC 17) with it.

While scrying with the crystal ball, you have **truesight** with a radius of 120 feet centered on the spell's sensor.

title: Cubic Gate
summary: A 3-inch cube with each face keyed to a different plane of existence, allowing you to open portals between planes.
content:
## Cubic Gate

*Wondrous item, legendary*

This cube is 3 inches across and radiates palpable magical energy. The six sides of the cube are each keyed to a different plane of existence, one of which is the **Material Plane**. The other sides are linked to planes determined by the DM.

You can use an action to press one side of the cube to cast the **gate** spell with it, opening a portal to the plane keyed to that side. Alternatively, if you use an action to press one side twice, you can cast the **plane shift** spell (save DC 17) with the cube and transport the targets to the plane keyed to that side.

The cube has **3 charges**. Each use of the cube expends 1 charge. The cube regains **1d3 expended charges** daily at dawn.

title: Defender
summary: A legendary sword with a +3 bonus that allows you to transfer some or all of that bonus to AC instead.
content:
## Defender

*Weapon (any sword), legendary (requires attunement)*

You gain a **+3 bonus to attack and damage rolls** made with this magic weapon.

The first time you attack with the sword on each of your turns, you can transfer some or all of the sword's bonus to your **Armor Class**, instead of using the bonus on any attacks that turn. For example, you could reduce the bonus to your attack and damage rolls to +1 and gain a +2 bonus to AC. The adjusted bonuses remain in effect until the start of your next turn, although you must hold the sword to gain a bonus to AC from it.

title: Hammer of Thunderbolts
summary: A massive magical maul that gains devastating power when combined with gauntlets of ogre power and a belt of giant strength.
content:
## Hammer of Thunderbolts

*Weapon (maul), legendary*

You gain a **+1 bonus to attack and damage rolls** made with this magic weapon.

**Giant's Bane (Requires Attunement).** You must be wearing a *belt of giant strength* and *gauntlets of ogre power* to attune to this weapon. The attunement ends if you take off either of those items. While attuned and holding this weapon, your **Strength score increases by 4** (to a maximum of 30). When you roll a 20 on an attack roll with this weapon against a **giant**, the giant must succeed on a **DC 17 Constitution saving throw** or die.

The hammer also has **5 charges**. While attuned to it, you can expend 1 charge and make a ranged weapon attack with the hammer, hurling it as if it had the thrown property with a normal range of 20 feet and a long range of 60 feet. If the attack hits, the hammer unleashes a **thunderclap** audible out to 300 feet. The target and every creature within 30 feet of it must succeed on a **DC 17 Constitution saving throw** or be **stunned until the end of your next turn**. The hammer regains **1d4 + 1 expended charges** daily at dawn.

title: Horn of Valhalla (Iron)
summary: An iron horn that summons 5d4+5 berserker warrior spirits from Ysgard to fight for you.
content:
## Horn of Valhalla (Iron)

*Wondrous item, legendary*

You can use an action to blow this horn. In response, **5d4 + 5 warrior spirits** from the plane of Ysgard appear within 60 feet of you. They use the **berserker** statistics, return to Ysgard after 1 hour or when they drop to 0 hit points, and are friendly to you and your companions. They obey any verbal commands you issue (no action required). If no commands are given, they defend themselves but otherwise take no actions.

Once the horn has been blown, it can't be used again for **7 days**.

The iron horn requires proficiency with **all simple weapons** to use. If you blow the horn without the required proficiency, the summoned berserkers attack you instead.

| Horn | Spirits | Proficiency Required |
|------|---------|---------------------|
| Silver | 2d4+2 | None |
| Brass | 3d4+3 | Simple weapons |
| Bronze | 4d4+4 | Medium armor |
| **Iron** | **5d4+5** | **All simple weapons** |

title: Iron Flask
summary: A small iron bottle that can capture and contain extraplanar creatures, binding them to your service when released.
content:
## Iron Flask

*Wondrous item, legendary*

This iron bottle has a brass stopper. You can use an action to speak the flask's command word, targeting a creature that you can see within 60 feet of you. If the target is native to a plane of existence other than the one you're on, the target must succeed on a **DC 17 Wisdom saving throw** or be trapped in the flask. If the target has been trapped by the flask before, it has **disadvantage** on the saving throw. Once trapped, a creature remains in the flask until released.

You can use an action to remove the flask's stopper and release the creature. It is friendly to you and your companions for **1 hour** and obeys your commands for that duration. If you give no commands or give it a command that is likely to result in its death, it defends itself but otherwise takes no actions. At the end of the duration, the creature acts in accordance with its normal disposition and alignment.

An *identify* spell reveals whether the flask is empty or occupied, and the type of creature contained.

title: Plate Armor of Etherealness
summary: Plate armor that allows you to become ethereal once per day, entering the Ethereal Plane.
content:
## Plate Armor of Etherealness

*Armor (plate), legendary (requires attunement)*

While you're wearing this armor, you can speak its command word as an action to gain the effect of the **etherealness** spell, which lasts for **10 minutes** or until you remove the armor or use an action to speak the command word again. This property of the armor can't be used again until the next **dawn**.

title: Potion of Giant Strength (Storm)
summary: A potion that sets your Strength score to 29 for 1 hour when consumed.
content:
## Potion of Giant Strength (Storm)

*Potion, legendary*

When you drink this potion, your **Strength score changes to 29** for **1 hour**. The potion has no effect on you if your Strength is equal to or greater than that score.

This potion's transparent liquid has floating in it a sliver of fingernail from a **storm giant**.

| Type | Strength | Rarity |
|------|----------|--------|
| Hill | 21 | Uncommon |
| Frost/Stone | 23 | Rare |
| Fire | 25 | Rare |
| Cloud | 27 | Very Rare |
| **Storm** | **29** | **Legendary** |

title: Ring of Djinni Summoning
summary: A ring that allows you to summon a djinni that serves you obediently for up to 1 hour per day.
content:
## Ring of Djinni Summoning

*Ring, legendary (requires attunement)*

While wearing this ring, you can speak its command word as an action to summon a particular **djinni** from the Elemental Plane of Air. The djinni appears in an unoccupied space you choose within 120 feet of you. It remains as long as you concentrate (as if concentrating on a spell), to a maximum of **1 hour**, or until it drops to 0 hit points. It then returns to its home plane.

While summoned, the djinni is **friendly** to you and your companions. It obeys any commands you give it, no matter what language you speak. If you fail to command it, the djinni defends itself against attackers but takes no other actions.

After the djinni departs, it can't be summoned again for **24 hours**, and the ring becomes nonmagical if the djinni dies.

title: Ring of Elemental Command
summary: A ring attuned to one of the four elements, granting resistance, spellcasting, and command over elementals of that type.
content:
## Ring of Elemental Command

*Ring, legendary (requires attunement)*

This ring is linked to one of the four Elemental Planes. The DM chooses or randomly determines the linked plane.

While wearing this ring, you have advantage on attack rolls against **elementals** from the linked plane, and they have disadvantage on attack rolls against you. You can also speak and understand the language of the linked element.

In addition, you have access to properties based on the linked plane. The ring has **5 charges** and regains **1d4 + 1 expended charges** daily at dawn.

**Ring of Air Elemental Command:** Resistance to lightning damage; *feather fall*, *gust of wind*, *wind wall* at will; *chain lightning*, *dominate monster* (air elemental) via charges.

**Ring of Earth Elemental Command:** Resistance to acid damage; *stone shape*, *stoneskin*, *wall of stone* at will; *earthquake*, *dominate monster* (earth elemental) via charges.

**Ring of Fire Elemental Command:** Resistance to fire damage; *burning hands*, *fireball*, *wall of fire* at will; *fire storm*, *dominate monster* (fire elemental) via charges.

**Ring of Water Elemental Command:** Resistance to cold damage; *create or destroy water*, *control water*, *ice storm*, *wall of ice* at will; *dominate monster* (water elemental) via charges. You can also walk on water and breathe underwater.

title: Ring of Spell Turning
summary: A ring that grants advantage on saving throws against spells and can reflect spells of 7th level or lower back at the caster.
content:
## Ring of Spell Turning

*Ring, legendary (requires attunement)*

While wearing this ring, you have **advantage on saving throws** against any spell that targets only you (not an area effect). In addition, if you roll a **20 for the save** and the spell is **7th level or lower**, the spell has no effect on you and instead targets the caster, using the slot level, spell save DC, attack bonus, and spellcasting ability of the caster.

title: Robe of the Archmagi
summary: A legendary robe for spellcasters that provides base AC 15, advantage on spell saves, and boosts spell save DC and attack rolls.
content:
## Robe of the Archmagi

*Wondrous item, legendary (requires attunement by a sorcerer, warlock, or wizard)*

This elegant garment is made from exquisite cloth and adorned with silvery runes. The robe's color corresponds to the alignment for which it was created (white for good, gray for neutral, black for evil). You can't attune to a robe that doesn't correspond to your alignment.

While wearing this robe, you gain the following benefits:
- Your **base AC is 15 + your Dexterity modifier** if you aren't wearing armor.
- You have **advantage on saving throws** against spells and other magical effects.
- Your **spell save DC** and **spell attack bonus** each increase by **2**.

title: Scarab of Protection
summary: A jeweled brooch that grants advantage on saving throws against spells and can absorb negative energy from necromancy spells and the undead.
content:
## Scarab of Protection

*Wondrous item, legendary (requires attunement)*

If you hold this medallion in your hand for 1 round, an inscription appears on its surface revealing its magical nature. It provides two benefits while you wear or carry it:

- You have **advantage on saving throws** against spells.
- The scarab has **12 charges**. If you fail a saving throw against a **necromancy** spell or a harmful effect originating from an undead creature, you can use your reaction to expend 1 charge and turn the failed save into a successful one. The scarab crumbles into powder and is destroyed when its last charge is expended.

title: Sovereign Glue
summary: A viscous adhesive that permanently bonds objects together and can only be undone by universal solvent or a wish spell.
content:
## Sovereign Glue

*Wondrous item, legendary*

This viscous, milky-white substance can form a permanent adhesive bond between any two objects. It must be stored in a jar or flask that has been coated inside with **oil of slipperiness**. When found, a container holds **1d6 + 1 ounces**.

One ounce of the glue can cover a **1-foot square surface**. The glue takes 1 minute to set. Once it has done so, the bond it creates can be broken only by the application of **universal solvent** or **oil of etherealness**, or with a **wish** spell.

title: Spell Scroll (9th Level)
summary: A scroll bearing a 9th-level spell that can be cast without components if the spell is on your class's spell list.
content:
## Spell Scroll (9th Level)

*Scroll, legendary*

A spell scroll bears the words of a single **9th-level** spell, written in a mystical cipher. If the spell is on your class's spell list, you can read the scroll and cast the spell without providing any material components. Otherwise, the scroll is unintelligible.

Casting the spell by reading the scroll requires the spell's normal casting time. Once the spell is cast, the words on the scroll fade and it crumbles to dust. If the casting is interrupted, the scroll is not lost.

If the spell is on your class's spell list but of a higher level than you can normally cast, you must make an **ability check using your spellcasting ability (DC 19)** to determine whether you cast it successfully. On a failed check, the spell disappears from the scroll with no other effect.

- **Save DC:** 19
- **Attack Bonus:** +11

title: Talisman of Pure Good
summary: A holy talisman usable only by good-aligned clerics or paladins that can open a flaming fissure to destroy evil creatures.
content:
## Talisman of Pure Good

*Wondrous item, legendary (requires attunement by a creature of good alignment)*

This talisman is a mighty symbol of goodness. A creature that is neither good nor evil in alignment takes **6d6 radiant damage** upon touching the talisman. An evil creature takes **8d6 radiant damage** upon touching the talisman. Either sort of creature takes the damage again each time it ends its turn holding or carrying the talisman.

If you are a **good cleric or paladin**, you can use the talisman as a holy symbol, and you gain a **+2 bonus to spell attack rolls** while you wear or hold it.

The talisman has **7 charges**. If you are wearing or holding it, you can use an action to expend 1 charge and choose one creature you can see on the ground within 120 feet of you. If the target is of **evil alignment**, a flaming fissure opens under the creature. The target must succeed on a **DC 20 Dexterity saving throw** or fall into the fissure and be **destroyed**, leaving no remains. The fissure then closes, leaving no trace. When you expend the last charge, the talisman disperses into motes of golden light and is destroyed.

title: Talisman of the Sphere
summary: A talisman that grants advantage on Intelligence (Arcana) checks to control a sphere of annihilation and increases movement distance.
content:
## Talisman of the Sphere

*Wondrous item, legendary (requires attunement)*

When you make an **Intelligence (Arcana) check** to control a **sphere of annihilation**, you do so with **advantage** and you gain a **+10 bonus** to the check. In addition, when you start your turn with control over a sphere of annihilation, you can use an action to levitate the sphere **10 feet plus an additional number of feet equal to 10 x your Intelligence modifier**.

title: Talisman of Ultimate Evil
summary: A vile talisman usable only by evil-aligned clerics or paladins that can open a flaming fissure to destroy good creatures.
content:
## Talisman of Ultimate Evil

*Wondrous item, legendary (requires attunement by a creature of evil alignment)*

This item symbolizes unrepentant evil. A creature that is neither good nor evil in alignment takes **6d6 necrotic damage** upon touching the talisman. A good creature takes **8d6 necrotic damage** upon touching the talisman. Either sort of creature takes the damage again each time it ends its turn holding or carrying the talisman.

If you are an **evil cleric or paladin**, you can use the talisman as a holy symbol, and you gain a **+2 bonus to spell attack rolls** while you wear or hold it.

The talisman has **6 charges**. If you are wearing or holding it, you can use an action to expend 1 charge and choose one creature you can see on the ground within 120 feet of you. If the target is of **good alignment**, a flaming fissure opens under the creature. The target must succeed on a **DC 20 Dexterity saving throw** or fall into the fissure and be **destroyed**, leaving no remains. The fissure then closes, leaving no trace. When you expend the last charge, the talisman dissolves into foul-smelling slime and is destroyed.

title: Universal Solvent
summary: A milky liquid that dissolves any adhesive it is applied to, including sovereign glue.
content:
## Universal Solvent

*Wondrous item, legendary*

This tube holds milky liquid with a strong alcohol smell. You can use an action to pour the contents of the tube onto a surface within reach. The liquid instantly dissolves up to **1 square foot** of adhesive it touches, including **sovereign glue**.

title: Well of Many Worlds
summary: A fine black cloth that opens a two-way portal to another plane of existence when placed on a solid surface.
content:
## Well of Many Worlds

*Wondrous item, legendary*

This fine black cloth, soft as silk, is folded up to the dimensions of a handkerchief. It unfolds into a circular sheet **6 feet in diameter**.

You can use an action to unfold and place the well of many worlds on a solid surface, whereupon it creates a **two-way portal** to another world or plane of existence. Each time the item opens a portal, the DM decides where it leads. You can use an action to close an open portal by taking hold of the edges of the cloth and folding it up. Once the well of many worlds has opened a portal, it can't do so again for **1d8 hours**.

title: Orb of Dragonkind
summary: An ancient artifact that can summon and command dragons, but carries a powerful curse that can dominate its wielder.
content:
## Orb of Dragonkind

*Wondrous item, artifact (requires attunement)*

Ages past, on the world of Krynn, elves and humans waged a terrible war against evil dragons. When the world seemed doomed, the wizards of the **Towers of High Sorcery** came together and worked their greatest magic, forging five *Orbs of Dragonkind* to help them defeat the dragons.

While attuned to an orb, you can use an action to peer into the orb's depths and speak its command word. You must then make a **DC 15 Charisma check**. On a success, you control the orb for as long as you remain attuned to it. On a failure, you become **charmed** by the orb for as long as you remain attuned to it.

While you are charmed by the orb, you can't voluntarily end your attunement to it, and the orb casts *suggestion* on you at will (save DC 18), urging you to work toward the evil ends it desires.

**Properties.** An orb has the following random properties:
- 2 minor beneficial properties
- 1 minor detrimental property
- 1 major detrimental property

**Spells.** The orb has 7 charges and regains 1d4 + 3 expended charges daily at dawn. While attuned, you can expend charges to cast: *cure wounds* (5th level, 3 charges), *daylight* (1 charge), *death ward* (2 charges), or *scrying* (3 charges, save DC 18).

**Call Dragons.** While you control the orb, you can use an action to cause the artifact to issue a telepathic call that extends in all directions for **40 miles**. Evil dragons in range feel compelled to come to the orb as soon as possible by the most direct route. Dragon ones affected by this call are aware of others drawn to the orb. Each dragon makes a **DC 18 Charisma saving throw** when arriving within 1 mile. On a failed save, a dragon is charmed by you for 1 hour.

**Destroying an Orb.** An orb can be destroyed by a creature that is charmed by the orb, who makes a successful DC 18 Charisma check. The orb then shatters.

title: Eye of Vecna
summary: The preserved eye of the archlich Vecna that grants truesight and powerful spellcasting when placed in an empty eye socket.
content:
## Eye of Vecna

*Wondrous item, artifact (requires attunement)*

Seldom is the name of **Vecna** combos spoken except in a hushed voice. Vecna was, in his time, one of the mightiest of all wizards. Through dark arts and conquest, he forged a terrible empire. For all his power, Vecna couldn't escape his own mortality. He began to fear death and undertook studies in the most forbidden of magical arts — necromancy, eventually transforming into a lich and later a god.

The **Eye of Vecna** is a preserved human eye, bloodshot and with a bright green iris. To attune to the eye, you must press it into your empty eye socket. The eye grafts itself to your head and remains there until you die or the eye is removed with a *wish* spell.

Once the eye is in place, you gain the following benefits:
- **Truesight** out to 240 feet
- **X-ray vision** — use an action to see through up to 1 foot of stone, 1 inch of common metal, or 3 feet of wood or dirt (not lead)
- **Advantage** on saving throws against spells and other magical effects

**Spells.** The eye has 8 charges and regains 1d4 + 4 expended charges daily at dawn. You can expend charges to cast: *clairvoyance* (2 charges), *crown of madness* (1 charge), *disintegrate* (4 charges), *dominate monster* (5 charges), or *eyebite* (4 charges).

**Properties.**
- 1 minor beneficial property
- 1 major beneficial property
- 1 minor detrimental property

If you possess both the Eye and Hand of Vecna, further powers are granted at the DM's discretion.

**Destroying the Eye.** The Eye of Vecna can be destroyed only if it is placed in the empty eye socket of the severed head of a hallowed saint and then set afire with holy fire.

title: Hand of Vecna
summary: The mummified left hand of the archlich Vecna that grants immense strength and devastating cold damage on touch.
content:
## Hand of Vecna

*Wondrous item, artifact (requires attunement)*

The **Hand of Vecna** is a mummified, blackened human left hand. To attune to the hand, you must lop off your left hand at the wrist and press the artifact against the stump. The hand grafts itself to your arm and becomes a functioning appendage. If the hand is ever removed, you die.

Once the hand is attached, you gain the following benefits:
- Your **Strength score becomes 20** (no effect if already 20 or higher)
- Any melee spell attack you make with the hand and any melee weapon attack made with a weapon held by it deals an extra **2d8 cold damage** on a hit
- The hand has **8 charges** and regains **1d4 + 4 charges** daily at dawn

**Spells.** You can expend charges to cast: *finger of death* (5 charges), *sleep* (1 charge), *slow* (2 charges), or *teleport* (3 charges).

**Properties.**
- 1 minor beneficial property
- 1 major beneficial property
- 1 minor detrimental property

If you possess both the Eye and Hand of Vecna, further powers are granted at the DM's discretion.

**Destroying the Hand.** The Hand of Vecna can be destroyed only if it is placed on the stump of the left arm of the hallowed saint whose eye socket holds the Eye of Vecna. If that saint is then resurrected, the Hand and Eye are both destroyed.

title: Sword of Kas
summary: The sentient longsword wielded by Vecna's lieutenant Kas, a +3 weapon that grants extra damage, improved criticals, and an obsessive hatred of Vecna.
content:
## Sword of Kas

*Weapon (longsword), artifact (requires attunement)*

When **Vecna** grew in power, he appointed an **inner circle of followers** whom he called combos the Trusted, and among them his lieutenant **Kas the Bloody-Handed**. Kas was gifted a powerful sword for his loyalty. The blade turned on its master, severing Vecna's left hand and gouging out his left eye before Kas was struck down. The **Sword of Kas** survives as a sentient weapon, ever seeking to destroy Vecna.

While attuned to the sword, you gain the following benefits:
- **+3 bonus** to attack and damage rolls
- **Critical hit** on a roll of 19 or 20
- Extra **2d10 slashing damage** against undead
- You can use your reaction to make a melee attack with the sword against any creature within reach that deals damage to you

**Properties.**
- 1 minor beneficial property
- 1 major beneficial property
- 1 minor detrimental property
- 1 major detrimental property

**Spirit of Kas.** While the sword is on your person, you add a **d10 to your initiative rolls**. You can also use the sword to cast *call lightning*, *divine word*, or *finger of death*. Once you use the sword to cast a spell, that spell can't be cast again from it until the next dawn.

**Sentience.** The Sword of Kas is a sentient, chaotic evil weapon with Intelligence 15, Wisdom 13, Charisma 16. It has hearing and darkvision out to 120 feet. It can communicate telepathically and speaks Common.

**Personality.** The sword's purpose is to destroy Vecna. It also seeks to destroy anyone using either the Eye or Hand of Vecna.

**Destroying the Sword.** A *wish* spell can destroy the Sword of Kas if the spell is spoken within 5 feet of the sword.

title: Book of Exalted Deeds
summary: A sacred tome that permanently increases Wisdom and grants powerful divine boons to good-aligned creatures who spend time studying it.
content:
## Book of Exalted Deeds

*Wondrous item, artifact (requires attunement by a creature of good alignment)*

The definitive treatise on all that is good in the multiverse, the **Book of Exalted Deeds** figures prominently in many religions. Rather than being a scripture devoted to a particular faith, the book's various authors filled the pages with their own vision of true virtue, providing guidance for defeating evil.

The book contains sacred writings that are beneficial to the virtuous. To attune to the book, a creature of good alignment must spend **48 hours** reading and studying it. Afterward, the creature gains the following benefits:

- **Wisdom score increases by 2** (maximum 24)
- **Halo** — you gain a glowing halo that sheds bright light in a 10-foot radius and dim light for an additional 10 feet. Fiends and undead within that bright light have **disadvantage** on attack rolls against you.
- **Enlightened Magic** — any spell slot you expend to cast a cleric or paladin spell counts as a spell slot **one level higher**.

**Properties.**
- 2 minor beneficial properties
- 2 major beneficial properties

Once you have read and studied the book, any non-good creature that attempts to read it takes **24d6 radiant damage**. This damage can't be reduced or prevented in any way. A creature reduced to 0 hit points by this damage disappears in a blinding flash and is destroyed, leaving its possessions behind.

**Destroying the Book.** It is rumored that the Book of Exalted Deeds can't be destroyed as long as good exists in the multiverse. If burned, the text reappears in a new book in another corner of the multiverse.

title: Book of Vile Darkness
summary: A tome of unspeakable evil that grants dark power to evil-aligned creatures who study it, including the ability to command undead and cast vile spells.
content:
## Book of Vile Darkness

*Wondrous item, artifact (requires attunement by a creature of evil alignment)*

The contents of this foul manuscript are the fundamental texts of evil. No moral argument waged against it can hope to overcome the corruption contained within its pages. To attune to the book, a creature of evil alignment must spend **80 hours** reading and studying it.

Afterward, the creature gains the following benefits:

- **Charisma score increases by 2** (maximum 24)
- **Dark Lore** — you can reference the book whenever you make an Intelligence check to recall information about some aspect of evil (such as lore about demons). When you do so, **double your proficiency bonus** on that check.
- **Dark Speech** — while carrying the book, you can use an action to cast *dominate monster* on an evil target (no spell slot or components required, save DC 18). You can't use this property again until the next dawn.
- **Vile Knowledge** — you can cast any spell contained in the book as if it were on your spell list (DM determines which spells are included).

**Properties.**
- 2 minor beneficial properties
- 2 major beneficial properties
- 1 minor detrimental property
- 1 major detrimental property

A non-evil creature that attunes to the book must make a **DC 17 Charisma saving throw**. On a failure, the creature's alignment changes to **neutral evil**.

**Destroying the Book.** The Book of Vile Darkness allows pages to be torn from it, but any evil lore contained on those pages finds its way back into the book eventually. If a **solar** tears the book in two, the book is destroyed for 1d100 years, after which it reforms in some dark corner of the multiverse.

title: Wand of Orcus
summary: The skull-topped wand of the demon prince Orcus that commands undead and can conjure legions of the dead.
content:
## Wand of Orcus

*Wand, artifact (requires attunement)*

The ghastly **Wand of Orcus** rarely leaves its master's side. Made from bones asite hard asite iron, the wand is topped with a magically enlarged skull that once belonged to a human hero slain by Orcus. The wand can be wielded as a **magic mace** that grants a **+3 bonus** to attack and damage rolls made with it. The wand deals an extra **2d12 necrotic damage** on a hit.

**Properties.**
- 2 minor beneficial properties
- 1 major beneficial property
- 2 minor detrimental properties
- 1 major detrimental property

**Spells.** The wand has **7 charges** and regains **1d4 + 3 charges** daily at dawn. While holding the wand, you can use an action to expend charges to cast: *animate dead* (1 charge), *blight* (2 charges), *circle of death* (3 charges), *finger of death* (3 charges), *power word kill* (4 charges), or *speak with dead* (1 charge).

**Call Undead.** While you are holding the wand, you can use an action to conjure **undead** creatures whose combined average hit points don't exceed 500. These undead magically rise up from the ground or otherwise form in unoccupied spaces within 300 feet of you and obey your commands until they are destroyed or until dawn. Once used, this property can't be used again until the next dawn.

**Sentience.** The Wand of Orcus is sentient, chaotic evil, with Intelligence 16, Wisdom 12, Charisma 16. It has hearing and darkvision out to 120 feet and can communicate telepathically.

**Destroying the Wand.** The Wand of Orcus can be broken by a creature of deity-level power. Such a creature can destroy the wand by expending a fragment of its own divine power.

title: Axe of the Dwarvish Lords
summary: A legendary dwarven artifact battleaxe that grants a +3 bonus, extra damage to goblinoids, and powerful dwarven abilities.
content:
## Axe of the Dwarvish Lords

*Weapon (battleaxe), artifact (requires attunement by a dwarf)*

Seeing the peril his people faced, a young dwarf prince went on a quest to find a weapon powerful enough to unite the dwarven clans. The legendary smith fashioned this mighty weapon as the symbol of dwarven unity and power.

While attuned to the axe, you gain the following benefits:
- **+3 bonus** to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon
- Extra **2d8 slashing damage** against **goblinoids** (goblins, hobgoblins, bugbears)
- You gain **darkvision** out to 60 feet (or extend existing darkvision by 60 feet)
- You gain **tremorsense** out to 60 feet while standing on stone
- You can speak **Dwarvish** and **Undercommon**
- **Conjure Earth Elemental** — you can use the axe to cast *conjure elemental* (earth elemental only) once per dawn
- **Travel the Depths** — you can use the axe to cast *teleport* once per 3 days, but the destination must be underground

**Properties.**
- 2 minor beneficial properties
- 1 major beneficial property
- 2 minor detrimental properties

**Curse.** The axe bears a curse that affects any non-dwarf that becomes attuned to it. Even if the attunement ends, the curse remains. With each passing day, the creature's physical appearance and stature become more dwarflike. After 7 days, the creature looks like a typical dwarf, but taller by 1 inch. This change has no effect on the creature's game statistics.

**Destroying the Axe.** The only way to destroy the axe is to melt it in the **Adamantine Forge** where it was created. It must remain in the hottest part of the forge for 50 years before it finally succumbs to the fire and is consumed.
